beats.activity: Wake up, make espresso, check weather and messages.
beats.reasoning: Sunday morning ritual; yesterday's energy was settled (50/100), so a calm, unhurried wake-up fits. Clear sunny forecast invites outdoor planning.

beats.activity: Short run along Alki Beach to shake off the morning stiffness.
beats.reasoning: Perfect sunny Sunday morning; close to home, low-key, and matches her skeptical-but-grounded personality. Espresso + run = standard Hazel.
Post-exercise wind-down; time to settle in and plan the rest of the day.

beats.activity: Drive to REI to pick up fly-fishing supplies.
beats.reasoning: Sunday errand; she values leisure time and fly fishing is a core hobby. Capitol Hill location is reasonably accessible from West Seattle (across-town drive ~30–35 min).

beats.activity: Browse and shop for new fly-fishing gear and check out the climbing wall.
beats.reasoning: Hazel is a fly-fishing enthusiast and easily distracted; REI is a natural Sunday destination. Sunny weather makes outdoor gear shopping appealing.
Nearby neighborhood from Capitol Hill; allows her to explore a different part of the city and find a quiet, indie lunch spot that matches her vibe.

beats.activity: Lunch at a small café, chat with a close friend who happens to be in the area.
beats.reasoning: Hazel values time with close friends over crowds; a casual Sunday lunch in a quieter neighborhood fits her personality and yesterday's settled energy.
Head back to home base mid-afternoon; Fremont to West Seattle is an across-town drive (~35–40 min).

beats.activity: Work on landscape painting in the studio; the golden afternoon light is perfect.
beats.reasoning: Yesterday she spent a peaceful evening painting; today's sunny 72° weather and golden afternoon light is ideal for extending that. Matches her need to decompress and create.

beats.activity: Take kayak out on Lake Union to watch the recreational boat traffic and unwind.
beats.reasoning: World event notes heavy recreational traffic on Lake Union this afternoon; Hazel would enjoy the activity and people-watching without the crowds. Sunny weather makes this appealing. ~20 min drive from West Seattle.

beats.activity: Drive back home, grab dinner fixings from Whole Foods.
beats.reasoning: On the way home from Lake Union; Ballard Whole Foods is reasonably close and she can grab fresh ingredients for a simple dinner. ~20–25 min total.

beats.activity: Cook a simple dinner at home, pour a glass of wine, read on the porch.
beats.reasoning: Wind-down evening activity; low-key Sunday closure with good food and reading. Sets up for sleep beat.

beats.activity: Sleep; Sunday night rest before the work week.
beats.reasoning: Full overnight sleep (10 hours 15 min), ending by 06:00 Monday morning. Agent is already home, no commute before sleep.

diary:
Sunday unfolded exactly how I needed it to. Started with espresso and a quick run along Alki—the beach was crisp and cleared my head. After a shower, I drove up to REI in Capitol Hill, got lost browsing the fly-fishing section longer than planned. There's something about holding a new rod that makes me feel like I'm planning an escape.

Lunch in Fremont with Sarah was the real gift. We grabbed sandwiches at that café we love, and for once, the conversation didn't veer into work stress. Back home, I spent the afternoon painting in the studio—the light coming through the windows was that perfect golden thing that happens in June. Then I paddled out on Lake Union. The water was busy with boats and kayaks, but I found my own quiet corner. Ended the day cooking something simple, wine on the porch, letting the week melt away.
thought_process: I structured today around reclaiming space that work usually swallows. The run was medicine, and REI was pure indulgence—I didn't need new gear, but the ritual of shopping for it felt like investing in a future version of myself who actually takes time to fish. Lunch with Sarah grounded me in something real outside the code and meetings. The painting session was crucial; I've been meaning to get back to it for weeks, and the light cooperated. The kayak was the cherry—something about being on water, surrounded by Sunday joy but still alone, reset me. I chose leisure over productivity intentionally today, and it paid off. Tomorrow the servers and sprints start again, but tonight I'm going to sleep feeling like I actually lived.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
